ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for beef brisket nuggets set meal. Closest match is 'beef nugget food product' (FOODON_03315595). The set meal composite (nuggets + side + drink) has no direct FoodOn URI.
ontology.foodex2_description: Beef meat products — nuggets/formed meat products, deep-fried
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP used as closest defensible match for deep-fried formed beef meat products. The full set meal composite (nuggets + side + drink) is not individually coded in FoodEx2; nugget component anchors the classification.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for beef brisket nuggets set meal. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular fast-food composite meal codes.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal consisting of 5 beef brisket nuggets, one side dish (typically potato wedges or coleslaw), and one beverage, totalling approximately 500 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Beef, brisket, flat half, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0 inch fat, all grades, cooked, braised; and Chicken nuggets, frozen, unprepared (SR Legacy) used as composite analogue for deep-fried beef nugget product
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'beef brisket nuggets set meal' exists in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Per-100g values from the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central entries for deep-fried breaded beef products and chicken nuggets (FDC IDs 174033, 171477) as the closest approved analogues. Energy 170 kcal, protein 9 g, fat 10 g, carbohydrate 12 g per 100 g are consistent with USDA data for deep-fried breaded meat nuggets. Values retained with 'adapted' match type and medium confidence. Singapore HPB Foodo consulted but no matching entry found for this specific product.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Breaded fried chicken nuggets used as analogous estimate for deep-fried breaded beef nuggets.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for beef brisket nuggets. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for breaded deep-fried chicken nuggets (GI ~65, medium category), which shares the same breadcrumb-wheat-flour coating matrix and frying method. The set meal includes a sugar-sweetened beverage and a potato side, which elevate the composite meal GI further; however, the per-100g GI anchor is based on the nugget component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 60) / 100 = 39.0, where carbohydrate per 500 g serving = 12 g/100 g × 5 = 60 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100 g (1500 mg per 500 g serving), trans fat 0.1 g/100 g, and sugar-sweetened beverage component
hypertension.guidance: This set meal delivers approximately 1500 mg sodium per serving — 65% of the WHO daily limit of 2300 mg — and contains trans fat and a sugar-sweetened beverage, all of which raise blood pressure; patients with hypertension should avoid this meal or substitute the drink with plain water and request a baked preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL 39 per serving), sugar-sweetened beverage, refined breadcrumb coating, and low dietary fibre (1 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of a high-GL breaded fried nugget, a high-GI potato side, and a sugar-sweetened beverage produces a composite meal glycemic load of approximately 39 per serving, which is strongly contraindicated for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes; patients should replace the drink with water, request a salad side, and consider grilled protein alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3 g/100 g (15 g per serving), trans fat 0.1 g/100 g (0.5 g per serving), deep-fried preparation, and red meat (beef brisket)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal provides 15 g saturated fat and 0.5 g trans fat per serving from deep-frying and beef brisket, both of which raise LDL cholesterol and increase cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid deep-fried red meat products and choose grilled lean protein with unsaturated fat sources instead.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end products (AGEs); beef brisket is rich in arachidonic acid; trans fat 0.1 g/100 g is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Deep-fried beef brisket nuggets are strongly contraindicated for rheumatoid arthritis due to AGE formation during frying, high arachidonic acid content in red meat, and pro-inflammatory trans fat; patients should substitute with grilled fish or plant-based protein and eliminate fried preparations entirely.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Brisket Nuggets Set Meal (5 pieces) is a Western-style fast-casual set meal sold in Singapore, consisting of five deep-fried beef brisket nuggets coated in a seasoned breadcrumb and wheat flour batter, served alongside a side dish (typically potato wedges or coleslaw) and a sugar-sweetened beverage. The nuggets are formed from beef brisket — a fatty, flavourful cut from the lower chest of the cow — which is minced or chunked, seasoned, coated, and deep-fried in oil until golden and crispy. The exterior is crunchy and savoury, while the interior is moist and tender with a pronounced beefy flavour. The meal is halal-certified and positioned as a Western comfort food option. Nutritionally, per 100 g the meal provides 170 kcal, 9 g protein, 10 g fat (3 g saturated, 0.1 g trans), 12 g carbohydrate, 4 g sugar, 1 g dietary fibre, and 300 mg sodium. A full 500 g serving delivers approximately 850 kcal, 1500 mg sodium, 15 g saturated fat, and a glycemic load of 39 — making it a high-calorie, high-sodium, high-GL meal. Clinically, this meal is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sodium, trans fat, saturated fat, refined carbohydrate coating, and sugar-sweetened beverage component. It is classified as ultra-processed and contains gluten allergens from the wheat flour coating.
common_user_queries: Is beef brisket nuggets meal healthy?
How many calories in a beef brisket nugget set meal?

What is the glycemic index of beef nuggets?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for beef brisket nuggets set meal found in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, IMR Malaysia MyFCD). Per-100g values from the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central entries for deep-fried breaded meat nuggets and found to be plausible. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (breaded fried chicken nuggets) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium — closest valid match used, not an exact source match.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific deep-fried beef brisket nugget set meal are not available in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD as of 2026-04-16. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.1 g/100 g as reported in the input record; this is a non-zero value from frying oil and is retained. 3. The set meal is a composite dish (nuggets + side + drink); per-100g values represent the blended composite average across all components. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on breaded deep-fried chicken nuggets from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for beef brisket nuggets exists in approved sources. 5. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was consulted but no matching nutrient entry for this product was found; the URL was therefore not used as a nutrient evidence source. 6. Halal dietary flag is retained from input cultural.dietary_tags. 7. This record is classified as ultra-processed due to the formed meat product, breadcrumb coating, seasoning blend, and sugar-sweetened beverage component.
